description: In native Telugu vocabulary, the mid-front vowel /e/ is realized as [ɛ] (open-mid) and the long mid-front vowel /eː/ is realized as [æː] (open-mid long). These allophonic realizations become phonemic when English loanwords are incorporated into the vocabulary.
sources: Krishnamurti, B. (2003). _The Dravidian Languages_. Cambridge University Press.
Ashton, E. O. (1947). _Telugu Grammar_. Longmans, Green and Co.
Sridhar, S. N. (1990). _Kannada and Telugu_. In _The Dravidian Languages_ (pp. 143-208). Routledge.
